---
title: "probit-mebart-demo"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{probit-mebart-demo}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(latex2exp)
```

# Generate Data

```{r}
# Set random seed
set.seed(0)

# Generate true data
x_true <- rnorm(100, 0.0, 0.3)
y_true <- as.numeric(x_true > 0.0)

# Add measurement error
x_meas <- x_true + rnorm(100, 0, 0.1)

# Randomly flip some of the binary outcomes
subset <- sample(1:100, 5)
y_meas <- y_true
y_meas[subset] <- y_meas[subset]*-1+1

# Collect data into df
df <- data.frame(x_true, y_true, x_meas, y_meas)
```

# Fit BART and meBART models


```{r}

mdl_meBART <- meBART::mebart_probit(df$x_meas, df$y_meas,
                     meas_error_sigma = matrix(0.1^2),
                     x_mu = matrix(0.0),
                     x_sigma = matrix(0.3^2))


mdl_BART <- BART::pbart(df$x_meas, df$y_meas)

```


```{r}

# make predictions for BART
xs <- mdl_BART$treedraws$cutpoints[[1]]
preds_BART <- predict(mdl_BART, data.frame(xs))
quantiles <- t(apply(preds_BART$prob.test, 2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.975)))

# Collect data into df
df2 <- data.frame(x=xs, # input X
                  probs=preds_BART$prob.test.mean, # Mean probability
                  z=preds_BART$prob.test.mean>0.5, # output prediction
                  lower = quantiles[, 1], # Probability lower bound
                  upper = quantiles[, 2]) # Probability upper bound

# Plot BART results
ggplot() +
    geom_point(data=df2, aes(x = x, y = probs, color=z)) + 
    geom_line(data=df2, aes(x = x, y = probs), linetype=2) + 
    geom_ribbon(data=df2, aes(x=x, ymin=lower, ymax=upper), alpha=0.1) +
    geom_point(data=df, aes(x=x_meas, y=y_meas, color=y_true==1)) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept=0.0) +
    labs(title = "BART Probit",
       x = "x",
       y = TeX("$p(Y=1)$")) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks=c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1))


```



```{r}

# make predictions for meBART
xs <- mdl_BART$treedraws$cutpoints[[1]]
preds_meBART <- predict(mdl_meBART, data.frame(xs))
quantiles <- t(apply(preds_meBART$prob.test, 2, quantile, probs = c(0.025, 0.975)))

# Collect data into df
df3 <- data.frame(x=xs,
                  probs=preds_meBART$prob.test.mean,
                  # y = y,
                  z=preds_meBART$prob.test.mean>0.5,
                  lower = quantiles[, 1],
                  upper = quantiles[, 2])

# Plot meBART results
ggplot() +
    geom_point(data=df3, aes(x = x, y = probs, color=z)) + 
    geom_line(data=df3, aes(x = x, y = probs), linetype=2) + 
    geom_ribbon(data=df3, aes(x=x, ymin=lower, ymax=upper), alpha=0.1) +
    geom_point(data=df, aes(x=x_meas, y=y_meas, color=y_true==1)) + 
    geom_vline(xintercept=0.0) +
    labs(title = "meBART Probit",
       x = "x",
       y = TeX("$p(Y=1)$"),
       color="BART Prediction") +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks=c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1))



```

# Plot Updated Estimates of X


```{r}

# Extract posterior draws of X from meBART model
x_draws <- mdl_meBART$x_draws[1,,]
x_draws_means <- rowMeans(x_draws)

df_xdraws <- data.frame(X = x_meas, X_true= x_true)
df_xdraws$x_true_est <- x_draws_means

df_xdraws <- df_xdraws[order(df_xdraws$X_true), ]
df_xdraws$y <- 1:100

df_xdraws2 <- reshape2::melt(df_xdraws, id="y")

xdraws_plot <- ggplot(df_xdraws2) +
    geom_point(aes(x=value, y=y, color=variable), 
               shape=19, 
               size=2) + 
    theme(axis.text.y=element_blank(),
          axis.title.y=element_blank(),
          axis.ticks.y=element_blank(),
          legend.position = "bottom") +
    labs(title=TeX("Estimation of True Value of $X_i$"), 
         x="X",
         color="Variable") +
    geom_segment(data=df_xdraws, 
                 aes(x=X, xend=x_true_est, y=y, yend=y), 
                 arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm"))) +
    # scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-1, 1), breaks = seq(-1.2, 1.2, 0.2)) +
    scale_color_discrete(labels = unname(TeX(c("Measured Value, $X_i^*$", "True Value, $X_i$", "Estimated True Value, $\\hat{X_i}$"))))


xdraws_plot
```
